name: Potential DLL Search Order Hijacking
notes:
This rule triggers when attempts to create a DLL file to a known desktop application dependencies folder such as Slack, Teams or OneDrive and by an unusual process. This may indicate an attempt to load a malicious module via DLL search order hijacking.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Tim Rauch (rule), Elastic (idea)
SIGMA Title: Potential Initial Access via DLL Search Order Hijacking
SIGMA Description: Detects attempts to create a DLL file to a known desktop application dependencies folder such as Slack, Teams or OneDrive and by an unusual process. This may indicate an attempt to load a malicious module via DLL search order hijacking.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Successful File Modification, Audit.Object Load Success, System.File Created

when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") in ('winword.exe', 'excel.exe', 'powerpnt.exe', 'MSACCESS.EXE', 'MSPUB.EXE', 'fltldr.exe', 'cmd.exe', 'certutil.exe', 'mshta.exe', 'cscript.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'curl.exe', 'powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe') AQL filter query
when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of dll

when the event matches (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\users\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\appdata\%' and (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\onedrive\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft onedrive\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\teams\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\local\slack\app-%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\local\programs\microsoft vs code\%')) AQL filter query

when the event matches ((LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE '%cmd.exe' and (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\users\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\appdata\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\onedrive\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\api-ms-win-core-%'))) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential DLL Search Order Hijacking on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Successful File Modification, Audit.Object Load Success, System.File Created

AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") in ('winword.exe', 'excel.exe', 'powerpnt.exe', 'MSACCESS.EXE', 'MSPUB.EXE', 'fltldr.exe', 'cmd.exe', 'certutil.exe', 'mshta.exe', 'cscript.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'curl.exe', 'powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe') AQL filter query
AND when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of dll

AND when the event matches (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\users\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\appdata\%' and (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\onedrive\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft onedrive\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\teams\%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\local\slack\app-%' or LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\local\programs\microsoft vs code\%')) AQL filter query

AND NOT when the event matches ((LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE '%cmd.exe' and (LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\users\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\appdata\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\microsoft\onedrive\%' and LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\api-ms-win-core-%'))) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential DLL Search Order Hijacking

Event description: Detects when attempts to create a DLL file to a known desktop application dependencies folder such as Slack, Teams or OneDrive and by an unusual process. This may indicate an attempt to load a malicious module via DLL search order hijacking.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
